title: Capnoides gebleri
taxonomy: Capnoides gebleri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as borage or forget-me-not family. It is part of the genus Capnoides, which consists of several species of plants. The plant is native to Europe and Asia, and its classification reflects its botanical characteristics and relationships with other plants in the same family. As part of the Boraginaceae family, Capnoides gebleri is closely related to other plants like Heliotropium and Eritrichium. The exact classification of the plant is subject to ongoing research and updates in botanical classification systems.

intro: Capnoides gebleri, also known as Gebler's capnoides, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate digestive issues and reduce the severity of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Capnoides gebleri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
